text: In-Context Learning (ICL) has been identified as a promising paradigm for large language models (LLMs) due to the recent speed of advancements in LLMs. ICL enables models to perform specific tasks using a few-shot context in a manner that is different from the few-shot learning by updating the model parameters. With the increasing relevance of ICL to the assessment and the extension of the LLMs’ capabilities, this paper surveys ICL from all aspects, including current research developments, challenges, and more. Specifically, we introduce ICL in a formal way and highlight its relationship to other learning methods, and further classify and evaluate numerous existing researches on ICL methods, such as the training methods, the prompt engineering, and theories. Additionally, we introduce several use cases such as data engineering and dynamic knowledge injection. Finally, we summarize the limitations of ICL and suggest the directions for future work. This survey aims to be the basis of further research into the mechanics and optimization of ICL.
